problem.passage:
31. Read <A> and <B> and follow the directions. [2.5점]
<A>
Consider the sentences in (1).
succeed. (1)a. John is sure that he will
b. John is sure to succeed.
c.*John is sure his success.
The example (1c) is ungrammatical, though its underlined
complement is legitimate. The ungrammaticality of (1c) is
attributable to the following constraint on NPs: NPs in
sentences should have a case either inherently or through
being assigned by finite inflection, a transitive verb, or a
preposition. Sentences with a caseless NP are ruled out.
In (1c) the underlined complement lacks its case assigner.
영 어 (14면
<B>
*The a. child is difficult to travel alone.
*The b. city’s recent dumping the garbage made people upset.
*I was pleased my sister to be pregnant. c.
*Not d. surprisingly did they miss the train.
*It turned out the suspect to be innocent. e.
(‘*’ indicates that the sentence is ungrammatical.)
Choose all the sentences in <B> which have a caseless overt
NP.
① a, c, d ② a, c, e
③ b, c, e ④ b, e
⑤ d, e
